title: hibbertia favieri
intro: Hibbertia favieri, also known as the yellow peely, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to the eastern coast of Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Hibbertia favieri are said to include antioxidant and anti-aging properties, as well as the ability to support immune function and skin health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Hibbertia favieri are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and address various health concerns.
